title: Public Recreation Grounds (Allocation, Development Conditions, Management and Control) Regulations, 1994
title_en: Public Recreation Grounds (Allocation, Development Conditions, Management and Control) Regulations, 1994

provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Public Recreation Grounds (Allocation, Development Conditions, Management and Control) Regulations.

In these Regulations— " public recreation grounds " means and includes all those areas which have been earmarked in any approved planning scheme as areas for the passive or active recreation of members of the public, whether or not the said areas have been declared to be public recreation grounds under section 4 of the Public Recreation Grounds Act 1 . 1 Cap. 320
Public recreation grounds may be allocated to a person, group of persons, club or institution other than the local government authority for care and maintenance.

Any person, group of persons, club or institution to whom a public recreation ground is allocated, shall ensure that the public recreation ground so allocated is open to use by all members of the public for all reasonable hours as determined by the local government authority, without constraints.

Where the Minister or local government authority is satisfied that no proper care or maintenance of a public recreation ground can be undertaken without adequate fencing, the Minister or Local Government Authority, may give permission to fence the boundaries of the public recreation ground and the fence shall be kept in good condition at all times.

Subject to the approval of the Minister, the Local Government Authority may permit a person, group of persons, club or institution to whom a public recreation ground is allocated to establish, maintain and control aquaria, pavilions, piers, dressing rooms, lavatories and such other buildings, structures or conveniences of any nature as the local government authority may consider to be necessary for the proper care and maintenance or for convenient use of the recreation ground by the public.

Public recreation grounds shall be allocated for an initial term of five years. Thereafter, depending on the opinion of the local government authority allocation may be extended for a further term not exceeding ten years, subject to renewal.

The Minister may require the Local Government Authority to attach any other development conditions to the allocation of a public recreation ground including, inter alia , conditions to regulate the size of permitted buildings or structures, type of building materials, number and position of exits, or for protecting natural water courses and water bodies, natural vegetation; or any other conditions that the local government authority may consider necessary for the proper care and maintenance of the public recreation ground, or any matter in accordance with section 26 of the Town and Country Planning Act 2 . 2 Cap. 355 History of this document 31 July 2002 this version Consolidation Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
